responseMessageContents.text: The user is seeing a Firestore connection error. Let me look at the current state of the project to understand what's happening.

This Firestore connection error typically appears in the web preview environment due to network restrictions. It doesn't affect the actual mobile app when scanned via QR code.

If you're seeing this on the mobile device too, check that:
1. Your device has a stable internet connection
2. Your Firebase project is active and not in a restricted state

Let me verify the Firebase config is correct:
The user shared a Firestore connection error. Let me check the Firebase config to make sure everything is properly set up.

The user shared a Firestore connection error in the console. This is a common issue in the web preview environment. The Firebase config looks correct - it's reading from environment variables properly.

This error is typically benign in the web preview and doesn't affect the mobile app. Let me give the user a concise explanation.

This is a known Firestore behavior in the web preview environment. The web sandbox has network restrictions that prevent direct connections to Firestore's backend. 

**On mobile (via QR code), this error should not appear** as long as your device has internet access.

If you're also seeing it on mobile:
1. Verify your Firebase project is active in the [Firebase Console](https://console.firebase.google.com)
2. Check that Firestore is enabled (not in deleted/paused state)
3. Ensure your Firestore security rules allow the operations you're performing

The web preview will automatically retry and work in offline mode until a connection is established. Your app data will sync once connectivity is restored. This is normal Firebase behavior and not a bug in your app code.

Is there a specific feature or flow that's not working because of this?
